chapter: Chapter 12: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

questions.question: Why does a compass needle deflect when placed near a current-carrying conductor?
questions.answer: Because the current produces a magnetic field that interacts with the magnetic needle.

questions.question: How can the direction of magnetic field around a straight conductor be determined?
questions.answer: By using the right-hand thumb rule.

questions.question: What would happen to the magnetic field if the direction of current in a conductor is reversed?
questions.answer: The direction of the magnetic field around the conductor reverses.

questions.question: Why are magnetic field lines circular around a straight current-carrying conductor?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic effect spreads equally in all directions around the conductor.

questions.question: Why does a solenoid produce a strong magnetic field inside it?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic fields of individual turns combine to form a strong uniform field.

questions.question: Why is a solenoid considered similar to a bar magnet?
questions.answer: Because it has distinct north and south poles and a similar magnetic field pattern.

questions.question: What will happen if the number of turns in a solenoid is increased?
questions.answer: The strength of the magnetic field increases.

questions.question: Why does a current-carrying conductor experience a force in a magnetic field?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic field interacts with the moving charges in the conductor.

questions.question: What determines the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor?
questions.answer: The direction of current and the direction of magnetic field.

questions.question: Why are the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger kept perpendicular in Fleming's left-hand rule?
questions.answer: To represent mutually perpendicular directions of force, magnetic field, and current.

questions.question: Why does the coil in an electric motor rotate continuously?
questions.answer: Because the split ring reverses the direction of current after every half rotation.

questions.question: What would happen if there were no split rings in an electric motor?
questions.answer: The coil would stop after half rotation.

questions.question: Why is mechanical energy required in a generator?
questions.answer: To rotate the coil and change the magnetic field around it.

questions.question: Why is induced current produced when a magnet moves inside a coil?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic field linked with the coil changes.

questions.question: Why does no induced current flow if the magnet is stationary inside the coil?
questions.answer: Because there is no change in magnetic field.

questions.question: Why is AC more suitable for long distance power transmission?
questions.answer: Because its voltage can be easily increased or decreased using transformers.

questions.question: Why are thick wires used in household electric circuits?
questions.answer: To safely carry large currents without overheating.

questions.question: Why does a fuse protect electrical appliances?
questions.answer: Because it melts and breaks the circuit when excessive current flows.

questions.question: Why is the earth wire connected to the metal body of appliances?
questions.answer: To transfer leakage current safely to the ground.

questions.question: Why does overloading occur in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: When too many appliances draw current from the same circuit.

questions.question: Why are magnetic field lines stronger near the conductor carrying current?
questions.answer: Because the magnetic field strength is greatest close to the conductor.

questions.question: Why do magnetic field lines form closed loops?
questions.answer: Because magnetic monopoles do not exist.

questions.question: Why is a soft iron core preferred for making electromagnets?
questions.answer: Because it can be magnetised strongly and demagnetised quickly.

questions.question: Why is a generator called the reverse of a motor?
questions.answer: Because it converts mechanical energy into electrical energy instead of electrical to mechanical.

questions.question: Why does the magnitude of induced current increase when the coil rotates faster in a generator?
questions.answer: Because the rate of change of magnetic field increases.
